mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no positive correlation with rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and rising recession probability, driving S&P 500 forward returns negative over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 65 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate a broad-based contraction in aggregate demand, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Joint negative momentum in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 20-day returns with a t-statistic greater than 1.5 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the index of industrial production reflects deteriorating manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and yields negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: INDPRO momentum shows positive or null correlation with 60-day forward returns during late-cycle expansion phases, or Rank IC drops below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real gross domestic product growth signals expanding economic activity and higher corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 growth rates exhibit a negative or statistically insignificant correlation with 60-day forward returns across multiple rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index exceeding 0.5 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 70 percent of months where CPIAUCSL MoM inflation exceeds 0.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between nonfarm payrolls growth and the unemployment rate, where payrolls rise while unemployment falls, confirms a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline fails to generate a positive Rank IC greater than 0.03 in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 1.5 million units, the housing sector enters a downturn that drags down construction spending and related consumer durables, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 60 percent of observations where HOUST remains below 1.5 million units during a housing downturn regime.
